title: Malva durieui
taxonomy: Malva durieui is classified in the family Malvaceae. This classification places it within the order Malvales, which includes other plants in the same family. Malva durieui is a species within the genus Malva, a group of plants commonly known as mallows. The plant's classification is further supported by its physical characteristics and genetic similarities to other members of the Malvaceae family. As a member of the Malvaceae family, Malva durieui shares many similarities with other plants in the order Malvales.
uses: Malva durieui has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Its leaves and stems are often used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems. Malva durieui is believed to have antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe and heal wounds. Additionally, it has been used as a treatment for fever and respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Malva durieui.

parts: Malva durieui is a species of the Malva genus, which is known to have medicinal properties. The parts of the plant that are typically used for medicinal purposes are the leaves and flowers. preparations: Malva durieui is used in herbal preparations to address various health issues. The leaves and flowers of the plant are typically harvested for medicinal use.
